name: feat-ESE-0012-04
title: Buffer sync: upload, dispatch, readback lifecycle
description:
Implement the buffer sync system: uploading CPU TypedArrays to GPU before dispatch, dispatching compute passes, and reading back GPU-written buffers to CPU TypedArrays after dispatch. Integrates with the existing pipeline phases via sync points.

Ownership model: a component buffer has one writer per phase region. GPU systems run in update, readback happens before postUpdate. CPU systems in preUpdate mark buffers dirty for upload.

Inspired by Bevy's extract pattern and bevy_hanabi's zero-sync-for-GPU-only data.
acceptanceCriteria:
- uploadBuffers(): copies dirty CPU TypedArrays to corresponding GPUBuffers via queue.writeBuffer
- dispatchKernel(): creates command encoder, compute pass, sets pipeline + bind group, dispatches workgroups, submits
- readbackBuffers(): maps GPU-written buffers back to CPU TypedArrays for components in the kernel write set
- createGpuSystem(): wraps upload-dispatch-readback into a SystemFn compatible with addSystem()
- GPU-authoritative components (marked as such) skip readback unless explicitly requested
- Unit tests for upload correctness, dispatch execution, readback correctness
demoDeliverable: Create a world with 1000 entities having Transform + Velocity. Register a CPU movementSystem AND a GPU movementSystem on the same world (different entity sets via tags). Tick the world 60 times. Console.log final positions from both CPU and GPU entity sets, showing both produced correct results. Show timing comparison.
testingScenarios:
- Upload: CPU array [1,2,3] to GPU buffer, readback matches [1,2,3]
- Dispatch: GPU kernel that doubles values, readback shows doubled values
- Dirty tracking: unchanged components are not re-uploaded
- Capacity growth: adding entities beyond initial capacity triggers buffer regrow + re-upload
- Concurrent access: CPU system reads Transform after GPU system writes it (readback completes before CPU phase)
- GPU-authoritative skip: particle component marked GPU-only is not read back each frame
stakeholderUnderstanding: Buffer sync is the glue between CPU and GPU worlds. It handles the upload→dispatch→readback lifecycle and wraps it into a standard SystemFn that plugs into our existing pipeline phases. The ownership model (GPU writes in update, CPU reads after readback in postUpdate) is what makes shared components like Transform safe. GPU-authoritative components skip readback for performance.
